scope: Complete retail Fallout: New Vegas data in flat OpenMW, beginning with a normal level-one Goodsprings session and ending with the retail intro and Doc Mitchell character creation.
greenRule: Every gameplay subsystem must pass on the same committed OpenMW binary after the normal load-game path loads exact Save330, with natural authored state, exact binary/config/save provenance, durable hashed artifacts, and no --start bootstrap, script-run placement, substitute player, staged references, actor neutralization, forced proof state, or unresolved visual review. Synthetic starts are diagnostic only and can never increase parity percentage.

provenanceGate.reason: No durable, hash-verifiable normal Save330 gameplay run exists on the current clean-recovery runtime. The prior acceptance ledger also referenced twelve evidence files that are absent on disk.
requirements: normal load-game path; no --start, --new-game, script-run, PositionCell, teleport, or proof bootstrap
committed engine source and exact binary SHA-256
exact input save SHA-256 and proof that the save was actually consumed
existing hashed stdout, frame statistics, screenshots/video, and result manifest
zero unresolved black/white, missing-geometry, shader, alpha, or material regression

invalidatedIncident.observedFailure: A visible OpenMW window rendered black-and-white garbage/pixels instead of a valid Fallout: New Vegas gameplay frame.
invalidatedIncident.accounting: The run was wrongly initiated as a hidden profiler probe. Windows CreateNoWindow hid only the console, not OpenMW's GUI render window. The exact monochrome pixel cause is not yet proven; the same log confirms forced shaders plus unsupported FNV alpha/color interpolators and texture stages. No visual, timing, streaming, or gameplay claim from this run is admissible.
containment: deleted the temporary synthetic crossing harness
rolled back every streaming/profile/preset/contract edit made in that turn
froze automated OpenMW launches pending explicit user authorization

normalSessionBaseline.failure: There is no current normal Save330-backed gameplay baseline. Prior files named normal-session were synthetic/proof harness outputs or are absent and cannot certify session state.
visualReview: fail: black-and-white garbage/pixels were visible in the invalidated 2026-07-18 synthetic launch
unproven: no admissible Save330-loaded frame exists for renderer review
unproven: player, actors, HUD/Pip-Boy, sky, world materials, alpha, and final color all require a normal-load proof

subsystems.reason: No current normal Save330 load proves the native Player, quest/world state, position, inventory, equipment, or faction state. The former passing evidence is absent and is revoked.
Level-one movement and camera machine gates pass, but scale and retail timing are not yet same-slice differential proven.
Synthetic radius-one startup loads are inadmissible. No natural boundary crossing has been measured after a normal Save330 load; the 2026-07-18 smoke run is explicitly discarded.
The former door audit artifacts are absent and cannot prove activation or return. Normal Save330-backed authored door traversal and latency are unproven.
The authored Easy Pete reference resolves naturally without neutralization; exhaustive duplicate/invisible identity and schedule coverage is incomplete.
Easy Pete skin/beard/hair/sideburn parity and the normal Player equipment/body presentation fail.
Bounded Easy Pete furniture evidence exists, but the natural-session timing and arbitrary activation path are not proven.

Renderer acceptance is red. The 2026-07-18 synthetic launch visibly produced black-and-white garbage/pixels, and no current normal Save330-loaded frame proves terrain, sky, clouds, weather, lighting, alpha, materials, image-space, or final color.
No same-slice retail/OpenMW sound event, asset, mix or timing ledger exists.

Static and isolated dialogue work exists, but no admissible normal Save330 run proves Easy Pete or any broad dialogue selection, facing, voice/LIP, result scripts, interruption behavior, or natural quest progression.
The implicit proof outfit is removed, but authored starting inventory/equipment and inventory interactions are not acceptance-proven, and the native capture still uses fallback HUD presentation.
Benny shooting, Doc Mitchell wake-up, SPECIAL, skills, traits, appearance, controls and completion are not implemented as an acceptance path.
The oracle runner is isolated, but exhaustive per-record, per-draw, pixel and timing coverage is not yet present.
The former FO3 control artifacts are absent; the control is revoked until durable hashed evidence is regenerated.
The former Morrowind control artifact is absent; the control is revoked until durable hashed evidence is regenerated.
